name: Oily Fluid
description:
Oily Fluid — decomposed from the legacy `OilyFluidGenerator` Rust struct into the curated primitive library. Two state-store feedback loops (color, velocity) carry the simulation across frames. Each frame: (a) `feedback_velocity` is downsampled 4× (`node.downsample`) then H+V Gaussian blurred at quarter-res, mirroring the legacy `cs_downsample → 2× separable blur` path; (b) the velocity step extracts per-channel curl forcing from |color|'s normalised gradients (rotated 90°) and advects the quarter-res blurred velocity using the full-res unblurred velocity as the displacement field, then damps and adds the forcing; (c) the color step advects color by the new velocity, mixes in two channels of simplex noise injection (z-axis driven by `system.generator_input.time × speed × 0.6` so the noise field evolves frame-to-frame, matching the legacy `noise_time` term), then carries the result back to the color feedback. Both advect timesteps are `speed × disp` via `node.math` so the outer-card Speed slider scales both advection and noise rate. The render layer offers 5 modes (Oil Slick / Flow Field / Height Map / PBR / Lines); only the selected one's producer subgraph runs each frame via mux short-circuit (`EffectNode::selected_input_branch`), and the result is graded by the decomposed Color Grade chain (`node.gain` → `node.saturation` → `node.hue_saturation` → `node.contrast` → `node.clamp_texture`, the used subset of the legacy `node.color_grade` kernel — colorize/tint and the amount crossfade were never wired here so they are dropped; the trailing clamp keeps the legacy `max(result, 0)` floor).

Known parity deltas vs legacy: (1) 1-frame state delay through `node.feedback`'s `late_capture` phase, matching legacy's same-frame ping-pong + swap, (2) ColorRamp / ChannelMix stubs mean the Flow Field warm-sepia tint is omitted — recover via the outer-card Hue / Saturation, (3) sim evolves from black via per-frame noise injection — takes a few seconds to build visible patterns, (4) state textures are rgba16float pending the Feedback compute-shader copy path that will lift the cross-format blit restriction so fp32 state can be opt-in without requiring every intermediate node in the loop to also be annotated fp32.

Graph organization: the 50 flat nodes are wrapped into ten labeled boxes that flatten to the exact same graph at load (byte-for-byte identical wiring, params, and shaders — grouping is presentation only, the runtime never sees a box). The two state textures `feedback_color` and `feedback_velocity` stay visible at the top level so both feedback loops read at a glance. The velocity loop is three boxes: Curl Forcing (derives the rotational forcing from the colour field's gradients), Smooth Velocity (4x downsample then separable Gaussian blur), and Advect Velocity (self-advect, damp, add the curl force). The colour loop is two: Inject Noise (time-driven two-channel simplex injection) and Advect Color (advect by the new velocity, fade, add noise). Render Modes is the one nested box — it holds the shared Surface base (`heightmap` -> `normal_main`) and the mode mux at its own level, fanning into four sub-boxes (Oil Slick, Height Map, PBR, Lines); the single-node Flow Field branch stays flat beside them. Color Grade is the final gain -> saturation -> hue -> contrast -> clamp chain. All sixteen performance bindings target inner nodes by stable nodeId, so every slider drives exactly what it drove before grouping.
